description: Grounding from fz-rh2.16.3: remaining artifact failures after callable-entry closure fixes are protocol dispatch callsites whose receiver type can match more than one impl. Example: fixtures/enumerable_protocol_dispatch/input.fz analyzes counted/1 once with a union receiver from list-pattern element flow, so Enumerable.count/1 has List, Range, and Map candidates. Current CallSiteSummary can name only one SelectedCallee; when semantic resolution sees multiple matches it emits no summary, leaving backend/materialization with a reachable DirectCall and no settled call edge. Goal: make protocol callsites with multiple possible impls correct by construction, either by producing an explicit protocol-dispatch artifact edge/matrix or by splitting activations before artifact closure. Signal: enum_map_family, enum_take_drop_split, enum_tier0, enumerable_protocol_dispatch, map_enumerable, membership_operator, and range_enumerable no longer fail with missing materialized/direct call edges and can be promoted or routed to golden decisions.
title: Compiler2 protocol dispatch: materialize multi-target call edges for union receivers
